case.focal_claim: … finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs [compared to villages dominated by upper caste].

execution_overview.overall_summary: Docker was unavailable, so the analysis was executed locally using Python after minor path handling and dependency fixes. Both Task1 and Task2 (Welch t-test of totinc by domlow) ran successfully. Results show significantly higher income in low-caste dominated villages (t=3.8350, p=0.0001363). Outputs written to data/outputs/results_12hio.txt.

deviations.description: Added local path fallback in the Python script to use the study data directory when /app/data is unavailable.
deviations.reason: Execution outside Docker required the script to reference the local data path.

deviations.description: Installed compatible versions of numpy, pandas, scipy, and pyreadstat in the local environment.
deviations.reason: Missing Python libraries in the local environment.

deviations.description: Executed locally rather than inside a Docker container due to Docker daemon unavailability.
deviations.reason: Docker build failed: connection refused to the Docker daemon.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Executed the planned Welch two-sample t-test of totinc by domlow with no controls, matching the planned path.

result_raw.result_text: Income is significantly higher in low-caste dominated villages (Welch t=3.8350, p=0.0001363; mean diff=3572.59).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The result supports the focal claim: low-caste households in low-caste dominated villages have substantially higher income.
